message:
fix(fcp-oauth): redact authorization URL + callback Debug leaks (br-8i1x4)

Closes flywheel_connectors-8i1x4 (P1 oauth-callback leak).

Reviewer flagged two Debug-side sibling leaks that commit 19487b91
(AuthorizationError redaction) did not cover:

1. AuthorizationSession::Debug at oauth2.rs:300-307 printed
   `authorization_url` verbatim. build_auth_url embeds the OAuth
   `state` parameter and the PKCE `code_challenge` directly in the
   URL's query string. `state` was already redacted as a named
   field above, but appeared again inside authorization_url, and
   code_challenge was fully exposed. Any tracing log or Debug print
   that dumped an AuthorizationSession leaked enough material for a
   CSRF-adjacent attacker to predict or replay the authorization
   challenge.

2. AuthorizationCallback::Debug at oauth2.rs:804-812 printed
   `error_description` and `error_uri` verbatim. Both fields come
   from the attacker-controlled OAuth callback URL (per my earlier
   19487b91 analysis on the same fields in AuthorizationError).
   Same log-injection / HTML-escape hazard; same fix pattern.

Fix (landed collaboratively — parallel agent seeded the Debug impls
and a new `redacted_authorization_debug_url` helper in my working
tree; I committed them after fixing the test assertions):

- AuthorizationSession::Debug now routes authorization_url through
  `redacted_authorization_debug_url`, which Url-parses and strips
  query + fragment so the scheme/host/path remain legible but state,
  code_challenge, redirect_uri, and any fragment-embedded material
  never reach Debug output. On parse failure (malformed URL), emit
  "[REDACTED]" outright rather than print an unparseable string.
- AuthorizationCallback::Debug now renders error_description and
  error_uri as "[REDACTED]" (matching the `code` and `state`
  arms already present). The short RFC 6749 §4.1.2.1 `error` enum
  token stays visible — same policy pinned in 19487b91.

Test fixes on top of the seeded diff:

- test_callback_debug used 1-letter sentinel values for `code` and
  `state` ("c" and "s"). Once `error: Some("access_denied")` was
  added to exercise the error-not-redacted path, both single-letter
  assertions (`!debug.contains("c")`, `!debug.contains("s")`)
  trivially fired on "access_denied" — a false regression. Replaced
  with distinctive multi-char sentinels (SECRET_CODE_7f4a /
  SECRET_STATE_b19c) and added error-message-carrying assertion
  messages so future regressions report the offending Debug string.
- test_authorization_session_debug_redacts_state_and_pkce now
  constructs an authorization_url that contains the PKCE code_challenge,
  state, client_id, and a fragment so every redaction surface is
  exercised.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib — 621 pass vs. 619 baseline
(net +2 passing from the new redaction tests); same 2 pre-existing
broken Display-assertion tests (state_mismatch_empty_strings,
test_request_token_debug — both documented baseline-equivalent in
the R2 P3 19487b91 session). Zero regression.

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose IMPLEMENTER (cross-review follow-up to 19487b91)
